meta_title: For Freelancers: API integration guide to Solve can't finish projects (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome can't finish projects. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: API Integration Guide to Solve Project Stalls
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on client projects. Master API integration with this advanced checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster and impress clients.
content.answer_block: To overcome project paralysis, treat API integrations as modular services rather than monolithic code blocks. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for automated implementation, you eliminate architectural friction, ensuring you ship robust, scalable features in hours—not weeks—keeping your freelance momentum high and your clients satisfied.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank IDE, the cursor blinking with rhythmic indifference. The client is asking for progress, but your architecture has hit a wall of complexity. Every API integration feels like a potential point of failure, and the fear of breaking production keeps you in a cycle of endless planning and zero execution.

It’s time to stop over-engineering and start shipping. This guide isn't about theory; it’s a high-octane workflow designed for the solo developer who needs to balance speed with professional-grade reliability. We’re stripping away the friction using modern AI agents to turn integration headaches into automated wins.

steps.title: Define the API Contract
steps.description: Before writing code, establish a strict schema. This prevents runtime errors and scope creep during the build phase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the API documentation provided at [URL]. Generate a TypeScript interface and a zod validation schema for all endpoints, ensuring strict type safety and error handling definitions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create types and schemas based on the API docs in the project root'

steps.title: Bootstrap Service Layer
steps.description: Create a decoupled service class to handle requests. This isolates the API logic from your UI components.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust API client class using Axios with interceptors for auth-token injection, logging, and exponential backoff retry logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'refactor the API client into a modular service class in src/services/api.ts'

steps.title: Implement Webhook Handlers
steps.description: Handle asynchronous events without blocking your main process. Use a queue-based approach for reliability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a secure webhook endpoint handler that verifies signatures, validates payloads against our schema, and queues events for background processing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a new webhook route handler with signature verification middleware'

steps.title: Automated Integration Testing
steps.description: Never ship an integration without automated verification. Use mock servers to simulate edge cases.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of integration tests using Vitest that mocks the API response and verifies our service handles 401, 429, and 500 status codes correctly.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate unit tests for the API service and run them to ensure 100% coverage'

steps.title: Production Deployment Pipeline
steps.description: Finalize your CI/CD configuration to ensure your integration survives the real world.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs our integration tests on every pull request and validates environment variables before deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure a CI/CD pipeline in .github/workflows/deploy.yml'

pro_tips: Always use a dedicated API proxy to mask your internal logic and add a layer of caching for high-frequency requests.
Implement circuit breakers in your service layer to prevent one failing third-party API from taking down your entire application.
Use environment-specific base URLs managed via CLI tools to avoid accidental production API calls during development.
Log every external request payload and response in a secondary database for post-mortem debugging of client issues.
When using Claude Code, always ask it to 'explain the reasoning' before it commits code to ensure you maintain full architectural control.
content.next_steps: Once the integration is live, set up monitoring alerts using tools like Sentry or Datadog to capture errors in real-time, then move on to optimizing your API response times.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools and the blueprint. Now, stop the cycle of unfinished projects and start delivering high-impact work that commands premium rates. The difference between a struggling freelancer and a top-tier builder is the speed of execution. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
